Title: The chemical compound ‘Heatin’ stimulates hypocotyl elongation and interferes with the Arabidopsis NIT1-subfamily of nitrilases

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcripts abundant in young seedling tissues
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcripts abundant in young seedling tissues
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive auxin promoting hypocotyl elongation and thermomorphogenesis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Redundantly contributes to Heatin-mediated hypocotyl elongation; aao1 aao2 double mutant is insensitive to Heatin while thermomorphogenesis remains intact
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis of aao1 single and aao1 aao2 double mutants; expression database support
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component to manipulate chemical responsiveness of growth; mechanism remains unresolved
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Contribution to auxin biosynthesis is debated; altering AAO1 together with AAO2 may impair Heatin responsiveness without abolishing basal warm-growth response
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper presents AAO1/AAO2 as redundant contributors to Heatin response, not as definitively established auxin biosynthetic enzymes. Included as Common Gene because it is an oxidase linked to metabolite conversion but not established as the focal pathway enzyme for the nutrient/metabolite endpoint in this study.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAO1 is expressed in young seedlings. 2. Single aao1 mutants remained largely responsive, but aao1 aao2 double mutants were insensitive to Heatin. 3. AAO1 therefore redundantly contributes to Heatin-mediated hypocotyl elongation through an unclear mechanism potentially linked to auxin metabolism.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcripts abundant in young seedling tissues
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcripts abundant in young seedling tissues
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive auxin promoting hypocotyl elongation and thermomorphogenesis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Redundantly contributes to Heatin-mediated hypocotyl elongation, with a possible prevalent role for AAO2; aao2 single mutants showed reduced sensitivity and the aao1 aao2 double mutant was insensitive to Heatin
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis of aao2 single mutants and aao1 aao2 double mutants; expression database support
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to alter Heatin responsiveness or auxin-linked growth chemistry in Brassicaceae
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mechanistic contribution to auxin biosynthesis remains debated
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study emphasizes a possible prevalent role for AAO2 among Arabidopsis aldehyde oxidases in the Heatin response, but exact metabolic mechanism remains unresolved.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAO2 is highly expressed in young seedlings. 2. aao2 mutants showed reduced sensitivity to Heatin, and loss of AAO1 plus AAO2 abolished Heatin responsiveness. 3. AAO2 thus redundantly supports Heatin-mediated elongation, potentially via an auxin-related oxidative route.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: indole-3-acetic acid; corresponding carboxylic acids plus ammonia
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes indole-3-acetonitrile and related nitriles to the corresponding carboxylic acids, including conversion of IAN to IAA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: mRNA levels respond to high temperature; not affected by Heatin at transcript level
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive auxin promoting hypocotyl elongation and thermomorphogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for full Heatin-mediated hypocotyl elongation; contributes to high temperature-induced hypocotyl elongation; Heatin inhibits NIT1-subfamily activity and causes IAN accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Chemical proteomics pulldown; nit1-3 mutant phenotyping; NIT1 overexpression phenotyping; in vitro recombinant enzyme assay with Heatin inhibition; in vivo IAN/IAA metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate auxin-linked growth and thermomorphogenesis in Brassicaceae; likely useful in combination with chemical modulation rather than simple overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: NIT1 overexpression caused shorter hypocotyls under control conditions; Heatin interaction may cause complex and indirect effects on IAA homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Recovered as a potential Heatin target in chemical proteomics. Authors propose direct interaction but state dedicated biochemical/biophysical proof is still needed. NIT1 is part of the Brassicaceae-specific NIT1-subfamily and distinct from NIT4.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NIT1 was identified among Heatin-enriched protein targets by chemical proteomics. 2. nit1-3 mutants showed reduced Heatin-induced hypocotyl elongation and reduced thermomorphogenic elongation. 3. Heatin inhibited recombinant NIT1-subfamily nitrilase activity and increased in vivo IAN levels, linking NIT1 to auxin-related metabolic effects.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: indole-3-acetic acid; corresponding carboxylic acids plus ammonia
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes indole-3-acetonitrile and other nitriles to corresponding carboxylic acids, contributing to IAA formation from IAN
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: mRNA levels respond to high temperature; not affected by Heatin at transcript level
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive auxin promoting hypocotyl elongation and thermomorphogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for full Heatin-mediated hypocotyl elongation; contributes to warm-temperature hypocotyl elongation; recombinant NIT2 activity is inhibited by Heatin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Chemical proteomics pulldown; nit2 knockout and NIT2 RNAi phenotyping; in vitro recombinant enzyme assay with Heatin inhibition; in vivo IAN/IAA metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for modulating thermomorphogenic growth and auxin metabolism in Brassicaceae
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss or knockdown reduced hypocotyl elongation responsiveness at warm temperature; metabolic effects are complex because IAA still increases upon Heatin treatment
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NIT2 displays atypical temperature sensitivity reported in prior work, which may be relevant for thermomorphogenesis. Recovered as a Heatin target candidate in proteomics.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NIT2 was identified as a Heatin-enriched candidate target. 2. nit2 knockout and RNAi lines showed reduced Heatin responsiveness and reduced thermomorphogenic elongation. 3. Heatin inhibited recombinant NIT2 activity, supporting a role in Heatin-associated auxin metabolic perturbation.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream precursor-supply branch for Brassicaceae-specific IAN-to-IAA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts tryptophan to indole-3-acetaldoxime, an upstream precursor of IAN and IAA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive auxin promoting hypocotyl elongation and thermomorphogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required upstream for normal high temperature-induced and Heatin-responsive hypocotyl elongation; cyp79b mutants showed reduced Heatin responsiveness
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping of cyp79b lines for warm-temperature and Heatin responses
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream lever for modifying auxin precursor flux in Brassicaceae
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced precursor supply decreases elongation responses
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as upstream enzyme in the Brassicaceae-specific pathway leading to IAN and then IAA. Included because mutant analysis in Results directly linked it to the Heatin/auxin metabolic response.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP79B2 functions upstream of the IAN/IAA route relevant to Heatin response. 2. cyp79b mutants had reduced hypocotyl elongation at warm temperature. 3. Heatin responsiveness was reduced in cyp79b mutants, indicating dependence on this auxin precursor pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream precursor-supply branch for Brassicaceae-specific IAN-to-IAA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts tryptophan to indole-3-acetaldoxime, an upstream precursor of IAN and IAA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive auxin promoting hypocotyl elongation and thermomorphogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes upstream to high temperature-induced and Heatin-responsive hypocotyl elongation through IAN/IAA precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping of cyp79b lines for warm-temperature and Heatin responses
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream target for tuning auxin precursor flux in Brassicaceae
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced precursor supply lowers elongation response
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included together with CYP79B2 because the Results tested cyp79b mutants directly and linked this step to Heatin-associated auxin metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP79B3 contributes to the upstream production of IAN precursors. 2. cyp79b mutants were compromised in warm-temperature hypocotyl elongation. 3. Reduced Heatin responsiveness in cyp79b backgrounds supports involvement of the IAN/IAA pathway.
